title: Check Privacy Settings
summary: Review and adjust what personal information apps and websites can access.
learnerPromise: You will be able to review what information apps and websites collect about you and adjust those settings to match your comfort level.
whyItMatters: Many apps and websites request access to your location, contacts, photos, and other personal data. Reviewing these settings helps you share only what you are comfortable with.
realLifeUses: You installed a new app and want to check what permissions it has
You read that a social media platform collects location data and want to turn it off
You want to limit targeted ads based on your browsing history
You are cleaning up old accounts and want to tighten privacy on the ones you still use
materialsNeeded: A phone, tablet, or computer with internet access
result: A list of privacy settings you reviewed and any changes you made, with reasons for each.
youWillLearn: How to find privacy and permission settings on your device and in your accounts
How to understand what different permissions mean — location, camera, contacts, and more
How to turn off permissions or data collection you do not want
How to limit ad tracking and personalized advertising
miniChallenge: Review the privacy settings for one app or website and turn off at least one permission or data collection setting you do not need.
steps: Choose one app or website account to review — start with one you use often
Open the app settings on your device or the account settings on the website
Find the privacy, permissions, or data section and read through what is currently allowed
Turn off any permissions or data collection that feel unnecessary — such as location access for a game
Write down or note what you changed so you can remember your choices
completionCriteria: You reviewed the privacy or permission settings for at least one app or account
You turned off at least one setting and can explain why you made that choice
commonProblems: An app stops working after you change permissions — you can turn specific permissions back on if needed
Privacy settings are hard to find — look for a 'Privacy' link in account menus or app settings
Unclear what a permission does — search the permission name online for a plain-language explanation
tips: A good rule of thumb: if an app does not need a permission to do its main job, consider turning it off
Check privacy settings every few months and after installing new apps
Use your device's privacy dashboard to see which apps have accessed your data recently
Be especially careful with apps that request access to contacts, microphone, or location
